name: Cajun Maquechou
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat bacon drippings in a heavy 12-inch skillet over medium heat.
2. Add corn, onion, garlic, and green pepper; cook until the onion is thoroughly wilted and transparent, about 10 minutes. Stir often to prevent sticking.
3. Add tomatoes, salt, black pepper, sugar, and cayenne; stir until combined.
4. Add broth. Reduce heat and barely simmer, stirring often, until the liquid has almost evaporated, about 30 minutes. The mixture will be thick and mushy.
5. Stir in milk; cook until reduced by half.
6. Increase heat slightly. In a small bowl, beat eggs until frothy. Stirring constantly, add to pan in a slow steady stream. Cook just to thicken, about 3 or 4 minutes.
7. Serve hot. Makes 4 to 6 servings.
